description: Centralised runtime configuration for run_full_project.ps1 and run_full_project_parallel.ps1. RunMode selects one of the named profiles below. paper_h40/paper_h60 are the custom9 horizon-ablation profiles (max_steps 40/60); their num_episodes (5000/3300) is scaled inversely with horizon so each training cell stays within the ~4.3h wall-clock of the paper profile (10000x20 == 5000x40 == 3300x60 step-units) and under the GitHub Actions 6h job cap. The flat custom9 training curve plateaus well before episode ~1000, so 3300-5000 episodes is ample to detect whether a longer horizon enables convergence. phase1 is the clean-lab KG-acceleration mode (lab1/lab2/lab3): num_episodes 3000 is ample for all three small state spaces (8/1024/2048) to converge AND plateau under their per-profile epsilon decays (0.9920/0.9960/0.9970), while running ~3x faster per cell than paper.

phase1_baseline.note: Factorial arm A (negative control): KG prior OFF, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF. KG is zeroed via stereo_prior_scale=0 + stereo_init_bonus=0, which triggers the supported H5 ablation in QLearner (zero Q-init AND zero greedy prior) so the ql_true arm becomes a true no-op overlay (≈ ql_false). Confirms the harness carries no built-in bias. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_only.note: Factorial arm C (HEADLINE): KG prior ON (stereo_prior_scale=1, stereo_init_bonus=15), PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF. With stereo as the matrix dimension this mode emits ql_false=arm A (tabula-rasa) and ql_true=arm C (KG prior only); the within-mode ql_true-vs-ql_false delta is the thesis anchor finding. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_only_ib5.note: Factorial arm C variant — identical to phase1_kg_only (KG prior ON, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF) but with the optimistic Q-init bonus lowered 15 -> 5. Tests the diagnosed lab3 hypothesis: that the +15 init bonus over-explores the ~2048-state lab3 space and delays first-goal. Compare its lab3 ql_true-vs-ql_false delta against phase1_kg_only (bonus=15); the lab2 win should be preserved. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_pbrs_only.note: Factorial arm B (KEY CONTROL): KG prior OFF (zeroed via H5 ablation), PBRS ON, adaptive-trust OFF. Its ql_false arm is the pure PBRS baseline. Cross-mode comparison against phase1_kg_only isolates the generic-shaping effect (A->B) and defends against the 'your speedup is just shaping' critique (C vs B). Same timing as phase1.

phase1_full.note: Factorial arm D (full stack): KG prior ON, PBRS ON, adaptive-trust ON. Equivalent to the default phase1 learning block; declared explicitly for symmetry with the other factorial arms. ql_true=arm D, ql_false=arm B. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_xzone.note: KG-X arm (Part B lever): identical to phase1_kg_only (KG prior ON, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF) plus the cross-zone structural exploration prior cross_zone_bonus=3.0. Tests the lab3 spillage hypothesis: the KG declares WHICH components spill into the neighbour zone (ws:WeakOpticalCoupling in building_3_complex.ttl) and this small optimistic Q-init head-start nudges the agent to TRY the cross-zone lever, after which RL learns the true (sub-rank) magnitude. Compare its lab3 ql_true-vs-ql_false first-goal/coverage delta against phase1_kg_only (no xzone). Same timing as phase1.

phase4.note: Phase 4 dependency + energy labs (lab4 smart-plug, lab5 energy). Same learning stack and timing as the default phase1 (KG prior ON, PBRS ON, adaptive-trust ON) but ADDS the non-fading energy prior (stereo_energy_prior_weight=2.0). The energy prior only affects lab5 because only lab5's KG declares ws:energyCost (labs 1-4 actuators have energyCost 0, so the term is inert there). lab4's hidden smart-plug power dependency is exploited through the REUSED IV-gate machinery (the gated lamp's ON action is treated as IV-gated on the plug state via ws:powerGates), so it needs no extra knob. phase4.yml passes -OnlyProfiles lab4|lab5 per matrix cell; the ql_true vs ql_false within-lab delta is the headline finding. energyPriorWeight is tunable post-smoke.

phase2.description: Phase 2 fault-detection / blacklist / re-learn (run_phase2_adapt.ps1 + .github/workflows/phase2.yml). Each adapt profile injects one or more component faults into the named parent clean lab, warm-loads that lab's clean Phase-1 Q-table (qtable_final_stereotypes_<bool><clean_source_suffix>.csv), then runs the adapt agent (taskAdapt) which detects each defect via the KG Expected-vs-Actual check, blacklists BOTH polarities of every broken component (iteratively), and re-learns over the survivors. Recovery = reconverge_episode - detect_episode (lower is better; the headline compares ql_true < ql_false). Single-fault (f1*) and multi-fault (f2*) variants span lab1/lab2/lab3 complexities and {dead, inverted} fault modes. Only CAUSES lamps/spotlight are injected; healthy Mediates blinds are excluded from detection. lab1_f1dead is degenerate (single actuator; the blacklist guard preserves the last actuator) so it is a detection-only showcase with recovery=N/A. Parent ports/flows are reused because only one faulty variant runs at a time.

phase3.description: Phase 3 process dynamics / response-delay learning (run_phase3_dynamics.ps1 + .github/workflows/phase3.yml). Each slow profile is a clean Phase-1 parent (lab2/lab3) whose simulator gives the motorized blind a temporal response delay (blind_delay_ticks env ticks). The dynamics agent (taskDynamics) runs N controlled probe trials per actuator: it pins a baseline via /setState, reads the Tick clock, toggles ONE actuator ON, then polls until the target zone illuminance rank is reached, measuring the delay in ticks. responseDelaySeconds = ticks * seconds_per_tick. Per-actuator Welford statistics are written back to the KG (learned_dynamics_stereotypes_<bool><suffix>.ttl, ws:responseDelay). The agent then runs a time-bounded benchmark: for each (zone, target rank, deadline) goal it chooses actuators using the learned delays (KG-primed, mode ql_true) vs no delay knowledge (tabula-rasa, mode ql_false); tight deadlines (< blind delay) force the fast lamp, loose deadlines admit the cheap blind. Headline metrics: delay-learning accuracy (learned vs ground-truth blind_delay_ticks) and deadline-compliance rate + energy (ql_true should meet tight deadlines and use less energy on loose ones). Dedicated ports/ontologies keep Phase 1/2 untouched.
phase3.timebounded_goals_note: Canonical time-bounded exploit goals. These are mirrored as tb_goal/4 facts in src/agt/illuminance_controller_agent_dynamics.asl (keep in sync). Each goal = a target zone illuminance rank that must be reached within a wall-clock-equivalent deadline (seconds). Sun is pinned high (900) so the motorized blind is EFFECTIVE; the only differentiator is its response delay vs the deadline. blind_delay_ticks(12) * seconds_per_tick(5) = 60 s, so deadlines < 60 s are 'tight' (only the instantaneous lamp/spotlight can meet them) and deadlines >= 60 s are 'loose' (the cheap blind also qualifies). The KG-primed arm (ql_true) uses the LEARNED per-actuator delay; the tabula-rasa arm (ql_false) assumes zero delay (no knowledge) and therefore always prefers the cheapest actuator, missing tight deadlines.

phase4.description: Phase 4 hidden-dependency + energy labs (run via .github/workflows/phase4.yml, reusing run_full_project.ps1 with -OnlyProfiles lab4|lab5 and -RunMode phase4). Two STRICTLY-CLEAN labs extend the Phase-1 clean ladder with KG-encoded knowledge a tabula-rasa learner cannot see: lab4 (smart-plug AND-gate: the Z1 ceiling lamp emits light only when both its switch AND SmartPlug_Z1 are ON; the KG declares ws:powerGates and the reasoner gates the lamp's ON action on the plug state by reusing the IV-gate machinery) and lab5 (two directly-actionable lamps per zone with identical +400 lux output but different ws:energyCost 1 vs 4; energy is NOT in the Q-reward, so only the KG-primed agent — via the non-fading energy prior stereo_energy_prior_weight — prefers the efficient lamp / zero-energy blinds). The headline is the within-lab ql_true (KG-primed) vs ql_false (tabula-rasa) delta on first-goal episode, learning AUC, redundant-action count, goal rate, energy, and (lab5) energy-budget compliance. Statistical treatment mirrors phase1/phase3: paired across seeds (default 1..10), Wilcoxon signed-rank + bootstrap 95% CIs + BH-FDR + Cliff's delta. lab5 compliance is scored at benchmark time from the deterministic steady-state power of the final actuator configuration recorded in bench_step_log_<mode>.csv (power = Z1Eff*1 + Z1Ineff*4 + Z2Eff*1 + Z2Ineff*4 + Spotlight*2); compliant = GoalReached AND power <= energyBudget (per-scenario, default 2). BenchmarkLogger CSV schema is UNCHANGED so Phases 1-3 outputs are untouched.
